data.title: Dutch lawmakers push forward with 36% tax law, offering exemptions for startup equity and non-investment properties.
data.description: Assets such as equity in a qualifying startup and physical property used for non-investment purposes were not subject to the 36% tax.

data.title: ADP Jobs Report beats forecasts, uncertainty on rate cut; S&P 500 stable. Fed outlook muddled.
data.description: There is uncertainty surrounding a potential rate cut in December. The latest ADP Jobs Report surpassed expectations, complicating the Federal Reserve's decision. The S&P 500 remains stable amidst this development.

data.title: Fidelity's Jurrien Timmer predicts Bitcoin's bottom at $60,000, expecting a new bull market to start around $67,589.
data.description: Fidelity's Jurrien Timmer recently declared that Bitcoin hit its cycle bottom at $60,000, indicating the start of a new bull market after a period of consolidation, with Bitcoin currently trading at $67,589. Timmer, Fidelity's director of global macro, had previously predicted this support zone when he mentioned the end of the four-year cycle bull market months ago. Despite uncertainty surrounding whether $60,000 is the absolute low, Timmer is optimistic that it is and expects the next cyclical bull market to kick off after a few months of consolidation. Timmer cited pattern recognition as the primary factor influencing his positive outlook. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.
